title: Extracellular DNA extraction from sediment using phosphate buffer and NucleoSpin® Soil kit (MACHEREY NAGEL)
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5c64v","text":"Sample preparation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"as7h0","text":"Desorption of DNA from mineral and organic particles","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8p411","text":"Contaminants elimination","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cqil5","text":"DNA fixation and washing","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d462b","text":"DNA elution","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ft8ul","text":" The following precautions must be applied :","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3kgsm","text":"    -    If possible, it is best to work in a room dedicated to rare and ancient DNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1j1h9","text":"    -    Wear gloves throughout the extraction process","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9uqk0","text":"    -    Clean the bench with DNA off","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5ilbf","text":"    -    Use tips with filters to avoid contaminations","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8d7mp","text":"    -    Include negative controls","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8oh7u","text":"    -    All steps have to be performed under a specific DNA-work station (sterile area equipped with air filtration and UV systems).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1rei","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1g4lm","text":"Materials preparation : ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bnesi","text":"    -    Clean a specific DNA - work station and apply the UV for 15min","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9dcq4","text":"    -    Sterilize spatulas, one spatula per sample. To limit risk of burns, during this step, you must wear a cotton blouse and do not use gloves","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d9a00","text":"            *    put a clean spatula in an ethanol solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ed2qi","text":"            *    with metal pincer, take the spatula","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bu1m8","text":"            *    pass the spatula through the flame, warning of the risk of burns","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bo191","text":"            *    place sterilize spatula in a clean metal or glass support","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"25bhr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4kq4v","text":"Samples preparation : ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c6geh","text":"    -    thaw sediment used for extraction, between 30min - 1h at room temperature or between 1h - 2h at +4°C (the time depends of the quantity of sediment and freezing temperature)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fng4f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fofn8","text":"Solutions preparation : ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fa8h0","text":"     -    Prepare saturated phosphate buffer (0.12 M Na2HPO4; pH ≈ 8)(according to Taberlet et al 2012)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fssgc","text":"            *    weigh the 2 compounds - see table below","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a8qev","text":"            *    transfer these compounds in a becher ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a676l","text":"            *    dissolve them in ultra pure water ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b3rne","text":"            *    check pH, it will be ≈8","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bvcqs","text":"            *    put solution into 1L graduated flask, make up to the mark with ultra pure water and mix","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"al3e","text":"            *    filter this solution on 0.2µM filter","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"em37e","text":"            *    store this buffer at +4°C, maximum one week ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3d5dn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"22dl3","text":"       ABCDE1NameLinear FormulaMolecular WeighFinal concentrationWeight for 1L of buffer2Sodium   phosphate monobasicNaH2PO4119.98 g/mol16.4mM1.97g3Sodium   phosphate dibasicNa2HPO4141.96 g/mol103.6mM14.7g","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":205}],"data":[]},{"key":"a3uhi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b0612","text":"    -    Check buffer SW2  - before the first utilisation, you need to add the indicate volume of ethanol (96 - 100%) to buffer SW2 concentrate and mark the label of the bottle to indicate that ethanol was added. This solution is stable at room temperature (18 - 25°C) for at least one year","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9f1th","text":"    -    Optional : Incubate the elution buffer SE at +50°C","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"_dt-h-center _dt-bold","0_1":"_dt-h-center _dt-bold","0_2":"_dt-h-center _dt-bold","0_3":"_dt-h-center _dt-bold","0_4":"_dt-h-center _dt-bold","1_0":"","1_1":"","1_2":"","1_3":"","1_4":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","2_2":"","2_3":"","2_4":""},"colWidths":[141,50,50,50,50],"data":[["Name","Linear Formula","Molecular Weigh","Final concentration","Weight for 1L of buffer"],["Sodium\n  phosphate monobasic","NaH2PO4","119.98 g/mol","16.4mM","1.97g"],["Sodium\n  phosphate dibasic","Na2HPO4","141.96 g/mol","103.6mM","14.7g"]],"guid":"BA3E5095799C11EAB44E0242AC110006","legend":{"blocks":[{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"8p1f1","text":"Informations on the compounds of saturated phosphate buffer","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":[]},"mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[23,23,23],"show_headers":true}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"enn4p","text":"The manufacturer advise to wear gloves and goggles and to flow the safety instructions for 2 reagents : ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6ne02","text":"    -    SB coutains Guanidinium thiocyanate 45 - 60%, ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c17c2","text":"         CAS number : CAS 593-84-0","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"992nv","text":"         Signal word : Irritant","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d9hmr","text":"         Hazard phrases : 302, 412","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"359m","text":"         Precaution phrases : 264W, 273, 301+312, 330","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"26dee","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bera7","text":"   -    SW1 coutains Guanidine hydrochloride 36 - 50% and 2 - propanol 20 - 35%","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8920s","text":"        CAS number : CAS 50-01-1, 67-63-0","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bhddi","text":"        Signal word : Irritant and Flammable","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b9kcf","text":"        Hazard phrases : 226, 302, 319, 336","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8fhk5","text":"        Precaution phrases : 210, 260D, 264W,  280sh, 301+312, 330","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dtav1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

   NucleoSpin® Soil kit (MACHEREY-NAGEL)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e460i","text":"    -    Ethanol (96 - 100%), molecular grade to prepare buffer SW2","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5ir1s","text":"    -    Saturated phosphate buffer (0.12 M Na2HPO4; pH ≈ 8), stored less than one week at +4°C","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2ej1k","text":"    -    tips with filter : ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"40eh1","text":"            \u003e 1000µL \u003e 12 tips per sample","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5hkhn","text":"            \u003e 100µL \u003e 1 tip per sample","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1bqoj","text":"    -    2 mL sterile microcentrifuge tube \u003e 4 per sample","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9aspp","text":"     (1 to transfert sediment - step 1; 1 to collect supernatant - step 3; 1 to collect solution after filter lysate - step 5; and 1 to elute DNA - step 10)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":5,"length":151}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1gk5o","text":"    -    parafilm","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ej09g","text":"    -    gloves","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Annotate 2mL tubes, one 2mL tube per sample Weigh annotated tubes, use the weigh scale (0.0001g of precision) → On your notebook, note the name of tubes and their weights With a sterilized spatula, homogenize a sediment and check that it is completely thawed (if not, wait for total defrost) Transfer ≈ 0.5g of sediment in the associated tube, 2 methods : - if the sediment is "compact", use a sterilized spatula to transfer the sediment - if the sediment countains a lot of water, use a 1000µL tip cuted with clean chisel and transfer sediment by pipetting few times. → On your notebook, note the used method and your comments about the sediment particularity Weigh the tube containing sediment → On your notebook, note the weight On the storage tube, make a cross to show that this tube countains tawed sediment thaw once : this is highly recommended to do not subject sediment to freeze-thaw cycles in particular if the aim is to perform downstream analyses on sed-DNA extracts. Keep this sediment at -40°C or -80°C for long term storage. → On your notebook, note where the sediments are stored Repeat this process for all the sediments that will be extracted during this session Add one volume of saturated phosphate buffer (0.12 M Na2HPO4; pH ≈ 8; stored less than one week at +4°C) for one volume of sediment e.g if you have weighed 0.5g of sediment, add 0.5mL of saturated phosphate buffer Close the cap and you can add parafilm to secure the closure
Attach 2mL tubes (containing sediment and P buffer) to agitator for rotation Agitate tubes at slow speed at Room temperature for 00:15:00
Centrifuge 10000 rpm, °C, 10 minutes Transfer the supernatant (without taking the pellet) to a new 2mL tube

Place a NucleoSpin® Inhibitor Removal Column (red ring) in a Collection Tube (2mL, lid) Load up to 650 µL of clear supernatant (obtained at the step 4) onto the filter Centrifuge 11000 x g, °C, 1 minute Repeat the load and the centrifuge step as many time as there is still some supernatant from step 4 to be filtered After each centrifugation, collect the filtered liquid in a clean tube : 1 single tube for all the filtration Discard the NucleoSpin® Inhibitor Removal Column Note : if a pellet is visible after the centrifugation, transfer the clear supernantant to a new collection tube (not provided in the kit) to get ride of this pellet, and continue with the clear supernatant
Add 250 µL of buffer SB Close the lid Vortex for 00:00:05 , make a brief centrifugation

Place a NucleoSpin® Soil Column (green ring) in a collection Tube ( 2mL) Load 550 µL of sample onto the column Centrifuge 11000 x g, °C, 1 minute Discard the flow through and place the column back into the collection tube Load the remaining sample onto the column Centrifuge 11000 x g, °C, 1 minute Discard the flow through and place the column back into the collection tube
Note : the same collection tube is used throughout the entire washing procedure to reduce plastic waste
1 st wash : Add 500 µL of buffer SB to the NucleoSpin® Soil Column Centrifuge 11000 x g, °C, 30 seconds Discard the flow through and place the column back into the collection tube
2 nd wash : Add 550 µL of buffer SW1 to the NucleoSpin® Soil Column Centrifuge 11000 x g, °C, 30 seconds Discard the flow through and place the column back into the collection tube

3 rd wash : Add 650 µL of buffer SW2 to the NucleoSpin® Soil Column Note : verify that ethanol was added to buffer SW2 during the first utilisation Centrifuge 11000 x g, °C, 30 seconds Discard the flow through and place the column back into the collection tube
4 th wash : Add 650 µL of buffer SW2 to the NucleoSpin® Soil Column Centrifuge 11000 x g, °C, 30 seconds Discard the flow through and place the column back into the collection tube
Centrifuge 11000 x g, °C, 2 minutes Note : if for any reason, the liquid in the collection tube has touched the NucleoSpin® Soil Column after drying step, discard flow through and centrifuge again

Place the NucleoSpin® Soil Column into a new microcentrifuge tube (not provided in the kit) Add 30 µL of buffer SE to the column Optional : to increase yield you can heat buffer SE at 50 °C Do not close the lid and incubate at Room temperature for 00:01:30 Close the lid and centrifuge 11000 x g, °C, 30 seconds Discard the NucleoSpin® Soil Column and keep the tube cointaining the DNA We recommend storing DNA frozen at -20°C until preparation of DNA library for HTS (or at -40°C to -80°C for longer storage)
Add 150 µL of buffer SL3 and vortex for 00:00:05 Incubate at 4 °C, in a fridge for 00:05:00 Centrifuge 11000 x g, °C, 1 minute
creator: Cecile Chardon
